summary: Example Fitness remediation tracker keeps privacy posture blocked until the known Supabase advisor findings are addressed and proved through reviewed evidence.

items.proposedNextAction: Pin secure search_path values on externally reachable SQL functions and capture reviewed proof after migration.
items.riskIfIgnored: Mutable search_path can widen the blast radius of function execution and blocks stronger privacy claims.

items.proposedNextAction: Enable leaked password protection in Supabase Auth and record reviewed operator proof before changing privacy posture.
items.riskIfIgnored: Weak password-leak safeguards keep account protection claims below proved status.

items.proposedNextAction: Refactor affected public RLS policies so auth context is initialized once per query path where practical.
items.riskIfIgnored: RLS performance costs can accumulate and make privacy-preserving access paths harder to sustain at scale.

items.proposedNextAction: Add covering indexes for the remaining foreign-key paths and capture migration proof in the owner repo.
items.riskIfIgnored: Join-heavy queries and policy evaluation paths may degrade under load.

items.proposedNextAction: Review index usage during the broader performance pass and drop only after owner-side confirmation.
items.riskIfIgnored: Unused indexes increase write overhead and complicate schema hygiene, but do not independently block privacy posture.

warnings: Example tracker demonstrates remediation ownership and evidence linkage only; it does not prove fixes.
blockers: Privacy claim posture remains blocked until reviewed remediation proof exists for the active security findings.

recommendedRegistryUpdates.summary: Replace generic privacy warning wording with explicit remediation-lane tracking language after the tracker is reviewed.
Point the Fitness scorecard next action at the explicit remediation tracker rather than generic blocked-posture wording.
